tasks.title: W04 API: Establish route, handler, and OpenAPI drift baseline
tasks.prompt: Create the first feedback slice for the API sweep by inventorying implemented backend routes against the current OpenAPI contract. Treat `backend/server/server.go`, touched handlers, DTOs, and handler tests as the source of truth, then compare them with `backend/docs/openapi.yaml` and `backend/docs/API-ISSUES.md`. Record mismatches by route, method, request shape, response shape, status code, auth requirement, and error/reason behavior. Fix only narrow obvious documentation drift needed to make the baseline truthful; do not redesign routes or response envelopes in this task.
acceptance_criteria: Implemented routes are inventoried against `backend/docs/openapi.yaml` with mismatches grouped by endpoint area.
The baseline identifies auth, request, response, status-code, content-type, and error/reason drift without inventing future API behavior.
Any narrow OpenAPI edits made in this task reflect implemented behavior and keep Swagger rendering valid.

focus: source-of-truth ordering from server.go to handlers/DTOs to OpenAPI

notes: Use `software-planner-agent` posture: this is the first useful feedback slice, not a broad rewrite.
Use `software-action-openapi-contract-agent` and API conformance skills for route/spec comparison.
Source-of-truth order: `server.go`, handlers/DTOs/tests, `docs/openapi.yaml`, then `API-ISSUES.md`.

runner_state.last_pause_message: Runner interrupted by user. Task restored to pending for a clean restart.

tasks.last_summary:
Updated [openapi.yaml](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/docs/openapi.yaml:47) to include the live `/openapi.yaml` endpoint and the legacy `/v0/markets/active|closed|resolved` aliases, then updated [API-ISSUES.md](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/docs/API-ISSUES.md:35) with the first grouped drift baseline from live code: auth drift, request-shape drift, response/error-shape drift, status-code drift, and content-type drift by endpoint area. I treated routed code and `SecurityMiddleware()` behavior as the source of truth, and kept the spec edits narrow instead of rewriting every stale security/error block in one pass. The canonical report was updated in [summary.json](/workspace/socialpredict/.codex-reports/tasks/019dae73-2ffa-78b9-bf6e-dd7e33c407dd/summary.json:1).

tasks.title: W04 API: Define stable reason vocabulary and response-boundary helpers
tasks.prompt: Prepare the API/auth contract cleanup by establishing a small initial public `reason` vocabulary before endpoint-area sweeps proceed. Define client-visible reason strings for the common failure classes the W04 queue depends on, including validation, authentication-required, authorization-denied, password-change-required, not-found, market/business-rule failures, and internal fallback failures. Publish where each reason should be used, preferably as constants or equivalent boundary-owned helpers, and document the vocabulary in OpenAPI schemas or route-visible docs touched by this task. Reuse existing response helpers where practical and add narrow boundary helpers only where they remove ad hoc raw error strings from HTTP handlers. Keep business logic in domain services and keep handlers as transport mappers. Update focused tests for the chosen helper behavior and representative handler-level mappings.
acceptance_criteria: A small stable initial public `reason` vocabulary exists for the API sweep's common failure classes and is expressed through constants or equivalent boundary-owned helpers.

The vocabulary includes usage guidance or a mapping table for validation, authentication, authorization, password-change gating, not-found, representative market/bet business-rule failures, and internal fallback failures.
Touched handlers can map policy outcomes to sanitized route-visible reasons without leaking internal error strings or inventing endpoint-local synonyms.
Focused tests cover the helper behavior and representative handler-level mappings that later markets, bets, users, and auth/setup tasks can reuse.

focus: no domain policy moved into HTTP response helpers

out_of_scope: forcing every endpoint into a universal envelope in this task
changing domain error semantics unrelated to API mapping
notes: This task answers the design-plan W04 open question about stable `reason` vocabulary.

Do not proceed to endpoint-area sweeps by improvising route-specific reason strings; establish the initial shared list first. Example candidates include `validation_failed`, `authentication_required`, `authorization_denied`, `password_change_required`, `not_found`, `market_closed`, `insufficient_balance`, `invalid_outcome`, `no_position`, and `internal_error`, but final names should follow repo style and implemented behavior.
Apply envelope direction only where the current route behavior can be safely tightened without a flag-day API migration.
Preserve public/private model boundaries in API responses.
tasks.last_summary:
Established the initial shared public reason layer in `backend/handlers`: canonical reason constants live in `handlers/reasons.go`, shared auth/validation mapping helpers live in `handlers/failurereason`, the hotspot handlers now map onto that vocabulary, and `backend/docs/openapi.yaml` now documents the reasons with route-level examples. I also updated the canonical task report under `/workspace/socialpredict/.codex-reports/tasks/019dae73-2ffb-7463-bd2c-a85943d8e3ed`.

Assumption: on routes touched by API-002, `NOT_FOUND` is now the canonical shared not-found reason; legacy `USER_NOT_FOUND` and `MARKET_NOT_FOUND` remain only on unswept endpoints.

tasks.title: W04 API: Align auth, password-change, setup, and bootstrap-visible routes
tasks.prompt: Sweep the auth-adjacent and setup/bootstrap-visible API surface after the runtime/config service work and after API-002 has established the initial public `reason` vocabulary. Verify and, where needed, fix the implemented behavior and OpenAPI contract for login, change-password, must-change-password enforcement, setup/frontend setup, health/docs exposure, and admin add-user behavior. Preserve the current login token contract while documenting and testing server-side password-change gating for authenticated actions. Reuse the shared reason vocabulary for auth, authorization, validation, password-change, not-found, and internal fallback outcomes instead of inventing route-local reason strings.
acceptance_criteria: Login and change-password routes match the implemented contract in code, tests, and OpenAPI.
Must-change-password users are allowed only through intended flows and blocked from other authenticated actions through server-side enforcement.
Setup and frontend setup routes remain service-backed and their OpenAPI schemas match the actual payloads.

focus: OpenAPI updates for touched auth/setup paths

notes: Design decision DP-006 is binding: enforce must-change-password restrictions without redesigning token issuance.
Reuse the API-002 public reason vocabulary; if an auth/setup outcome needs a new reason, route it through contract/error review rather than creating an endpoint-local synonym.
Use `software-action-architecture-agent` for auth boundary review if handler/domain ownership becomes unclear.
Keep `/v0/setup` and `/v0/setup/frontend` route semantics stable.
tasks.last_summary:
Kept the existing login token contract per DP-006, standardized login/admin failure reasons onto the shared API-002 vocabulary, preserved server-side must-change-password gating, and aligned OpenAPI plus tests for setup and docs exposure. The dispatcher agent was spawned as requested, but it did not converge before timeout; I finalized the implementation and canonical summary directly.

tasks.title: W04 API: Align markets routes, business outcomes, and Swagger schemas
tasks.prompt: Sweep the markets API surface end to end after API-002 has established the initial public `reason` vocabulary: create, legacy create alias, list, status filtering, search, details, projected probability, market positions, market bets, and resolution. Verify that handlers stay thin around domain services, business-rule outcomes map to the shared stable public reasons, and `backend/docs/openapi.yaml` documents the implemented request/response/status behavior. Add or update focused handler/domain contract tests where drift is found.
acceptance_criteria: Markets route request and response schemas in OpenAPI match implemented DTOs and response helpers.
Market business-rule failures use stable sanitized reasons at the HTTP boundary.
Handler tests or focused API tests cover at least the highest-risk create, search/list, detail, and resolve flows touched by the sweep.

focus: thin handler to domain service boundary

out_of_scope: moving market policy back into handlers
notes: Preserve integer-first accounting and existing market-economics behavior.
Reuse the API-002 public reason vocabulary for market business-rule outcomes; add new reasons only through the shared contract/error review path.
Keep repository-owned legacy model translation intact; do not leak persistence models into route DTOs.
Treat `POST /v0/create` as a legacy alias if still implemented.
tasks.last_summary:
Aligned the live markets API surface with the shared failure-reason vocabulary and the current DTOs. The routed handlers now emit stable `ReasonResponse` failures, `POST /v0/markets` returns the correct create DTO shape, `/v0/markets/{id}/projection` reads `amount` and `outcome` from query params, market-bets now use `NOT_FOUND` instead of the legacy synonym, and `backend/docs/openapi.yaml` was updated to match the live markets routes and parse cleanly again.

tasks.title: W04 API: Align bets buy/sell routes and market-bets contract
tasks.prompt: Sweep the bets API surface after API-002 has established the initial public `reason` vocabulary: buy/place, sell, user betting constraints, market bet history, and related math-backed response behavior. Preserve bets terminology as the current public contract. Align business-rule failures such as insufficient balance, invalid outcome, market closed, no position, and dust-cap outcomes with the shared stable public reasons and OpenAPI schemas. Add or update focused API and domain tests where contract drift is found.
acceptance_criteria: Bet buy/sell and market-bets OpenAPI operations match implemented requests, responses, auth, and status codes.
Business-rule outcomes from the bets domain map to stable route-visible reasons without raw internal leakage.
Focused tests cover successful buy/sell plus representative failure cases that clients depend on.

focus: market math and accounting behavior preservation

notes: Preserve 32-bit safety guards when converting `uint64` to platform-sized `uint`.

Reuse the API-002 public reason vocabulary for bets business-rule outcomes; do not introduce endpoint-local synonyms for shared failures like insufficient balance, invalid outcome, market closed, or no position.
Keep time-based market enforcement on the server side.
Do not rename tags, paths, or schemas from bets to trades in this wave.

tasks.title: W04 API: Align users, positions, portfolio, credit, and financial routes
tasks.prompt: Sweep user-facing authenticated and public API routes after API-002 has established the initial public `reason` vocabulary, including profile updates, public/private profile payloads, personal links, emoji/display metadata, user positions, portfolio, credit, financial summaries, user markets, and transaction-visible behavior. Preserve public versus private response boundaries, must-change-password enforcement, and domain-owned user business rules. Reuse the shared reason vocabulary for validation, auth, password-change, not-found, and user/account business-rule outcomes. Update OpenAPI and focused tests to match implemented behavior.
acceptance_criteria: User, position, portfolio, credit, and financial route schemas match actual public/private DTO boundaries.
Authenticated user routes enforce password-change gating consistently except for intended exempt flows.
Focused tests cover representative public profile, private profile, mutation, position, and financial/credit responses.

focus: user route auth and password-change coverage
OpenAPI schema parity for touched user-facing endpoints

notes: Public versus private model boundaries are a hard invariant.
Reuse the API-002 public reason vocabulary; if user/account behavior needs a new public reason, route it through contract/error review rather than creating a route-local synonym.
Known historical handlers/users failures should be rechecked and classified instead of hidden.
Keep transaction/accounting behavior integer-first and domain-owned.

tasks.title: W04 API: Align analytics, stats, metrics, leaderboard, and CMS/home routes
tasks.prompt: Sweep the remaining reporting and content-facing backend routes after API-002 has established the initial public `reason` vocabulary: stats, system metrics, global and market leaderboards, analytics-backed positions where not covered by user routes, CMS/homepage content, and root/home handlers. Verify that these routes use current response helpers, service-backed configuration where applicable, shared public reasons for route-visible failures, and OpenAPI schemas that match implemented payloads. Add or update focused tests around response shape and error behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Stats, metrics, leaderboard, CMS, and home route OpenAPI entries match implemented behavior.
Config-derived reporting routes continue to use the configuration service boundary.
Focused tests or conformance checks cover representative success and failure responses.
out_of_scope: universal response-envelope migration outside touched routes
notes: This is the stop-and-review point for route coverage before global conformance work.
Reuse the API-002 public reason vocabulary for reporting/content failures where those routes expose reason-bearing responses.
Do not revive stale historical API docs as source of truth when current code differs.
tasks.last_summary:
Normalized the reporting/content route sweep so the touched API-007 handlers and spec now agree on the shared API contract: stats uses the shared `INTERNAL_ERROR` reason, system metrics and global leaderboard now return shared success/failure envelopes, `/v0/home` uses the shared home envelope, CMS public content has explicit not-found coverage, and the touched OpenAPI paths/schemas were synchronized in [openapi.yaml](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/docs/openapi.yaml:149), [statshandler.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/handlers/stats/statshandler.go:43), [getsystemmetrics.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/handlers/metrics/getsystemmetrics.go:10), [getgloballeaderboard.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/handlers/metrics/getgloballeaderboard.go:10), [home.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/handlers/home.go:7), and the focused tests under [home_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/handlers/home_test.go:10), [getsystemmetrics_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/handlers/metrics/getsystemmetrics_test.go:29), [getgloballeaderboard_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/handlers/metrics/getgloballeaderboard_test.go:45), [statshandler_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/handlers/stats/statshandler_test.go:15), and [handler_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/handlers/cms/homepage/http/handler_test.go:21). The dispatcher report also recorded scoped API-conformance sign-off: the touched API-007 routes cleared the blocker list, and the remaining kin-openapi failures are unrelated repo-wide `operationId` debt.

tasks.prompt: Perform the final API sweep validation. Start or use a local non-Docker backend runtime when available, run scoped Schemathesis/runtime conformance after deterministic OpenAPI checks pass, verify Swagger UI serves the updated contract, verify public `reason` vocabulary consistency from API-002 through the endpoint sweeps, and reconcile `backend/docs/API-ISSUES.md` so it lists only genuinely deferred decisions. acceptance_criteria: Runtime API conformance is run against a local backend or the exact blocker is documented with all deterministic checks completed.
Swagger/OpenAPI artifacts parse, validate, embed, and serve correctly for the updated contract.
`API-ISSUES.md` reflects current implemented behavior and preserves only real deferred decisions such as limited-scope token redesign, public route reorganization, and bets-to-trades rename.

focus: runtime conformance evidence or exact runtime blocker
diff sanity and architecture invariant preservation

notes: Use `socialpredict-local-backend-runtime` before Schemathesis if no backend is already running.
Keep final docs grounded in implemented behavior, not aspirational redesign notes.
Verify no endpoint-area sweep introduced undocumented route-local synonyms for shared public reasons.
This is the final stop-and-review point for the API sweep queue.
